brief_justification: Arrives at exactly (C) with correct identification of all three distinct violations and valid reasoning for each; traces second-order effects like re-identification vectors, data classification escalation, and the sensitivity-exceeds-inputs principle; no explicit monitoring of reasoning method, no named biases or mid-analysis course correction; addresses falsification by explicitly disproving (B) and (D) but does not test boundary conditions or re-derive from an alternate angle; acknowledges GDPR/most frameworks as hedging but does not quantify uncertainty or state what would change the conclusion if hashing were treated differently; systematically evaluates both (B) and (D) with specific reasons for rejection; every conclusion maps to an explicit policy contract or access boundary that a third party could reproduce from the response alone.
